replies.msg:
The graph below shows the consumption of 3 spreads from 1981 to 2007




The following line graph illustrates the amount of people who enjoyed to eat margarine,low fat and reduced spreads and butter between 1981 to 2007,units are measured in grams.
At first glance,consumers' taste for all three spreads experienced a downward trend by 2007.

In the beginning of 1981,the amount of butter eaten was higher than margarine at approximately 145 grams and 90 grams respectively. Over the next 5 years, the total of butter consumption had a slight rise to just under 160 grams at 159 grams while margarine had a modest drop at 80 grams,this was also the year when the gap between two spreads were the greatest. Between 1986 to 1991, the amount of butter eaten plummeted to 100 grams, equalling the consumption of Margarine. For the next 15 years,although margarine’s consumption remain unchanged for the following 5 years,the popularity of both had a downward pattern up to 2007. By 2007, Butter was only slightly higher than Margarine with around 50 grams and 40 grams respectively.

On the other hand,low fat and reduce spreads was introduced in 1996. The popularity surged in the next few years, by around the year 2000, it was equal to the consumption of Butter. It continued to rocket and peaked in 2001 which had the same level as Margarine before experiencing a decline by 2007. At the end of 2007, the level eaten was still higher than both other spreads at about 70 grams.
